docs.title: MongoTUI
docs.text: A modern terminal UI for MongoDB administration Like Compass, but for your terminal.

docs.title: What is MongoTUI?
docs.text: MongoTUI is a terminal-based MongoDB administration tool built with Python, Textual, and PyMongo. It provides a Compass-like experience entirely in your terminal — perfect for servers, SSH sessions, and environments where GUI tools can't be installed.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• :material-database: Database Management Browse databases, collections, documents. Query with filters. Run aggregation pipelines.

  • :material-account-group: User Administration Create users, reset passwords, grant and revoke roles with a built-in role picker.

  • :material-format-list-bulleted: Index Management View, create, and drop indexes. Support for search indexes and vector search.

  • :material-chart-line: Live Monitoring Real-time graphs for connections, operations per second, and memory usage.

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text: pip install mongotui

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Interactive mode
mongotui

# Direct connection
mongotui -u "mongodb://admin:admin@localhost:27017/?authSource=admin"

docs.title: Why MongoTUI?
docs.text: Use Case MongoTUI Compass mongosh SSH access ✅ ❌ ✅ Visual UI ✅ ✅ ❌ No install needed on server ✅ (pip) ❌ ❌ User management UI ✅ ✅ ❌ Live monitoring ✅ ✅ ❌ Lightweight ✅ ❌ ✅

docs.title: Running a pipeline
docs.text:
1. Select a collection from the sidebar
  2. Navigate to the Aggregation tab
  3. Enter a pipeline as a JSON array
  4. Click Run Pipeline

docs.title: Count by field
docs.text: [{"$group": {"_id": "$status", "count": {"$sum": 1}}}]

docs.title: Filter and project
docs.text:
[
  {"$match": {"age": {"$gte": 21}}},
  {"$project": {"name": 1, "age": 1, "_id": 0}}
]

docs.title: Top 5 by score
docs.text:
[
  {"$sort": {"score": -1}},
  {"$limit": 5}
]

docs.title: Lookup (join)
docs.text:
[
  {"$lookup": {
    "from": "orders",
    "localField": "_id",
    "foreignField": "userId",
    "as": "orders"
  }},
  {"$project": {"name": 1, "orderCount": {"$size": "$orders"}}}
]

docs.title: Unwind arrays
docs.text:
[
  {"$unwind": "$tags"},
  {"$group": {"_id": "$tags", "count": {"$sum": 1}}},
  {"$sort": {"count": -1}}
]

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Results are limited to 100 rows in the display
  • The pipeline runs on the currently selected collection
  • Supports all standard MongoDB aggregation stages

docs.title: Browsing
docs.text:
Click Databases in the sidebar to see all databases with:

  • Collection count
  • Data size (MB)
  • Storage size (MB)

Click a specific database to expand and see its collections.

docs.title: Creating a database
docs.text:
Click Create DB in the Databases tab. Enter:

  • Database name
  • Initial collection name (required — MongoDB creates a database when its first collection is created)

docs.title: Dropping a database
docs.text:
Select a database in the table and click Drop DB. Confirm the deletion.

Warning

Dropping a database permanently deletes all collections and documents within it.

docs.title: Collections
docs.text:
Click a database in the sidebar to see its collections with:

  • Document count
  • Size (KB)

docs.title: Documents
docs.text:
Click a collection to browse its documents. The Documents tab shows:

  • First 50 documents in a table
  • A JSON filter input for querying
  • Click a row to see the full formatted document

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
Enter a JSON filter and click Find:

{"status": "active", "age": {"$gte": 21}}

Leave empty to show all documents.

docs.title: Viewing indexes
docs.text:
Select a collection from the sidebar. Navigate to the Indexes tab to see:

  • Index name
  • Keys and direction
  • Unique flag
  • Size (KB)
  • Total index size for the collection

docs.title: Creating an index
docs.text:
Click Create and enter fields in the format:

name:1,age:-1
  • 1 = ascending
  • -1 = descending

Optionally set as unique.

docs.title: Dropping an index
docs.text:
Select an index and click Drop.

Note

The _id_ index cannot be dropped.

docs.title: Search Indexes
docs.text: MongoTUI supports creating Atlas Search and Vector Search indexes.

docs.title: Text search index
docs.text:
Click + Search and configure:

  • Index name
  • Index type: Search (text)
  • Mapping: Dynamic (all fields) or Static (specify fields)
  • Analyzer: defaults to lucene.standard

docs.title: Vector search index
docs.text:
Click + Search and configure:

  • Index type: Vector Search
  • Vector field: the field containing embeddings (e.g. embedding)
  • Dimensions: vector size (e.g. 1536 for OpenAI, 768 for Cohere)
  • Similarity: cosine, euclidean, or dotProduct
  • Filter fields: optional fields for pre-filtering results

docs.title: Dropping search indexes
docs.text: Select a search index in the lower table and click x Search.

docs.title: Server Overview
docs.text:
The Overview tab shows:

Metric Description Host Server hostname Version MongoDB version Uptime Time since server start Connections Current / available Memory Resident and virtual (MB) Storage Engine WiredTiger, etc. Operations Insert, query, update, delete counts Replica Set Name and member count (or Standalone) Databases Total count

docs.title: Live Performance Graphs
docs.text:
Click Performance in the sidebar to see real-time graphs updating every 2 seconds:

  • Connections — active client connections over time
  • Operations / 2s — rate of insert + query + update + delete
  • Memory (MB) — resident memory usage trend

Keeps 60 data points (2 minutes of history).

docs.title: Replica Set Status
docs.text:
If connected to a replica set, the Overview shows member count. The replica set info includes:

  • Set name
  • Member states (PRIMARY, SECONDARY, ARBITER)
  • Health status
  • Uptime per member
  • Replication lag

docs.title: Viewing users
docs.text: Click Users in the sidebar or navigate to the Users tab. Shows all users with their database and assigned roles.

docs.title: Creating a user
docs.text:
Click Create and fill in:

  • Username
  • Password
  • Role (dropdown with all built-in MongoDB roles)
  • Database (defaults to admin)

docs.title: Resetting passwords
docs.text: Select a user, click Password, enter the new password.

docs.title: Granting roles
docs.text: Select a user, click Grant, pick a role from the dropdown, specify the database.

docs.title: Revoking roles
docs.text: Select a user, click Revoke, pick the role to remove.

docs.title: Deleting a user
docs.text: Select a user, click Delete, confirm.

docs.title: Built-in roles
docs.text:
MongoTUI includes all standard MongoDB roles in the dropdown:

Category Roles Database read, readWrite, dbAdmin, dbOwner, userAdmin Cluster clusterAdmin, clusterManager, clusterMonitor, hostManager All-DB readAnyDatabase, readWriteAnyDatabase, dbAdminAnyDatabase, userAdminAnyDatabase Backup backup, restore Super root

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • MongoDB 4.4+ (standalone, replica set, or Atlas)

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text: pip install mongotui

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DevOpsPack/mongotui.git
cd mongotui
pip install -e .

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text: mongotui --help

docs.title: Start MongoDB locally
docs.text:
docker compose up -d

This starts a MongoDB 7 instance with credentials admin:admin.

docs.title: Connect
docs.text:
mongotui -u "mongodb://admin:admin@localhost:27017/?authSource=admin"

Or run mongotui without arguments to get the interactive connection screen.

docs.title: Navigate
docs.text:
After connecting you'll see the dashboard with:

  • Sidebar — tree navigation for databases, collections, and features
  • Tabs — Overview, Databases, Collections, Documents, Indexes, Users, Performance

Click a database in the sidebar to see its collections. Click a collection to browse documents.

docs.title: Key actions
docs.text: Action How Browse databases Click "Databases" in sidebar View documents Click a collection in sidebar Query documents Enter JSON filter in Documents tab, click Find Create user Go to Users tab, click Create Monitor server Click "Performance" in sidebar Refresh Press r Quit Press q or Ctrl+C

docs.title: Command Line Arguments
docs.text: Argument Short Description --uri -u MongoDB connection URI (skips connect screen)

docs.title: Connection URI Format
docs.text: mongodb://[username:password@]host[:port][/database][?options]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Local without auth
mongotui -u "mongodb://localhost:27017"

# Local with auth
mongotui -u "mongodb://admin:admin@localhost:27017/?authSource=admin"

# Replica set
mongotui -u "mongodb://host1:27017,host2:27017,host3:27017/?replicaSet=rs0&authSource=admin"

# Atlas
mongotui -u "mongodb+srv://user:pass@cluster.mongodb.net/?authSource=admin"

docs.title: Important options
docs.text: Option Description authSource=admin Database to authenticate against (usually admin) replicaSet=name Replica set name directConnection=true Connect to a single node directly tls=true Enable TLS/SSL

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
The project includes a docker-compose.yml for local testing:

docker compose up -d
mongotui -u "mongodb://admin:admin@localhost:27017/?authSource=admin"

docs.title: Global
docs.text: Key Action q Quit Ctrl+C Force quit r Refresh all data Tab Switch focus between panels

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Key Action ↑ / ↓ Navigate tree or table rows Enter Select / expand tree node Escape Close modal

docs.title: Modals
docs.text: Key Action Escape Cancel and close Enter Submit (when button focused) Tab Move between fields
